reasons: Franco can suppress Obsidia before she stacks enough Bone Shards or after she commits with Hunt of Bone.
Suppression is valuable because Obsidia's threat depends on continuous movement and basic-attack uptime.

proof.summary: Obsidia wants to kite and attack while moving, while Franco can lock her with suppression and create a burst window.
worksBestWhen: Franco catches Obsidia before Phantom Shadowmeld.
Franco controls fog paths around Obsidia's farming or rotation route.
failureCases: Obsidia avoids hook with Shadowmeld timing.

reasons: Kaja can punish Obsidia's forward tether or aggressive marksman positioning.
Dragging Obsidia disrupts her preferred spacing and can remove her from safe attack range.

proof.summary: Obsidia wants to maintain movement-based DPS uptime, while Kaja can suppress and reposition her into a punish area.
worksBestWhen: Kaja waits for Obsidia to step forward.
Obsidia has no nearby peel or Shadowmeld escape timing.
Kaja's team is ready to burst immediately.
failureCases: Kaja is zoned before reaching Obsidia.
Obsidia keeps safe range and never commits.
Kaja suppresses while Obsidia's team can counter-engage.

reasons: Natalia's Smoke Bomb gives her a strong short window against basic-attack reliant marksmen.
Obsidia still has mobility, so Natalia must catch her before Shadowmeld or after Obsidia commits forward.

proof.summary: Obsidia relies on basic attacks plus Bone Shards for damage, while Natalia can create an anti-basic-attack zone and burst her during a close-range ambush.
worksBestWhen: Natalia attacks from fog before Obsidia reacts.
Obsidia's Shadowmeld is down or forced defensively.
Natalia can finish the burst before Obsidia kites out.
failureCases: Obsidia keeps distance and kites out Smoke Bomb.
Natalia enters through visible paths and gets focused.
Obsidia has peel or vision protection around her.

reasons: Saber can punish Obsidia if she is isolated and cannot Shadowmeld away before Triple Sweep connects.
This counter is timing-dependent because Obsidia has strong movement tools.

proof.summary: Obsidia wants to kite with mobility and moving attacks, while Saber can punish her during a no-escape or mispositioned window with targeted airborne burst.
worksBestWhen: Saber waits for Shadowmeld to be used.
Saber has enough burst to finish the punish window.
failureCases: Saber engages while Obsidia can avoid or outspace him.
Obsidia's team protects her during Saber's commit.
Saber reveals himself too early and Obsidia repositions.

reasons: Belerick can punish Obsidia's repeated basic-attack damage pattern better than many fragile frontliners.
This gives the team a stable frontline answer against Obsidia's sustained marksman output.

proof.summary: Obsidia wants continuous basic-attack uptime with Bone Shards, while Belerick can absorb and punish repeated attack patterns in front-to-back fights.
worksBestWhen: Belerick forces Obsidia to hit him first.
His team follows when Obsidia is slowed or controlled.
Belerick has item timing to survive repeated marksman damage.
failureCases: Obsidia ignores Belerick and targets backline heroes.
Belerick is kited without reaching control range.
Obsidia's team peels Belerick away before he can protect carries.
